doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_get_si: round an MPFR value to the nearest signed 64-bit integer per rnd, return as bigint. The TS port THROWS MPFRError('EPREC') on NaN / ±Inf / out-of-range result rather than returning a saturated sentinel — the locked schema (src/core.ts) has no ERANGE flag, so silent saturation would hide bugs at the trust boundary. The 'rounded to 0' edge case where the value's magnitude is < 1 is special-cased (substrate's roundMantissa requires outPrec >= 1).
divergence_from_c: C returns LONG_MAX/LONG_MIN/0 with the ERANGE side-channel flag on NaN/Inf/overflow; TS throws MPFRError('EPREC') because the locked schema has no flag surface and silent saturation hides bugs. Return type is `bigint` rather than `number` because LONG_MAX = 2^63 - 1 exceeds Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER = 2^53 - 1 and silent precision loss would defeat the purpose of the conversion. NaN/Inf cases are EXCLUDED from the golden because the harness can't grade expected-throw cases; coverage of those domain errors is by unit-test inspection of src/ops/get_si.ts.
refs: mpfr/src/get_si.c L25–L91 — the C reference: dispatches on mpfr_fits_slong_p, special-cases zero, mpfr_inits a 63-bit scratch and calls mpfr_rint to round per rnd, then extracts the integer.
mpfr/src/fits_s.h — the underlying mpfr_fits_slong_p logic for the range gate.
src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts — shared substrate; the integer-rounding path uses outPrec = exp to align the LSB to the units position.
src/ops/get_d.ts — sibling get-family op; that one saturates because IEEE 754 has Infinity, but the int target has no analog, hence the throw divergence here.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' — NaN ≠ NaN (we throw rather than return a flagged 0); rounding-mode count is FIVE.
